summary: Sudan is experiencing one of the fastest-growing displacement crises in the world due to widespread conflict between rival factions. The violence has devastated agricultural production and severely limited humanitarian access, pushing millions toward famine conditions, particularly in Darfur and Khartoum.

Surging gang violence in Port-au-Prince has completely disrupted supply chains and forced hundreds of thousands from their homes. Health systems are collapsing, and there is a critical shortage of clean water, leading to localized cholera outbreaks amid soaring inflation.

Non-state armed groups continue to blockade major towns, completely cutting off commercial supply lines. The resulting acute food insecurity is compounded by a lack of basic services in blockaded areas, forcing reliance on extremely expensive humanitarian airlifts that lack sufficient funding.
